# CI tooling pins — used as a pip CONSTRAINTS file (`pip install -c`), never
# installed directly. Workflows install the package from pyproject.toml and
# this file pins the exact versions the suite is verified against, so CI runs
# are reproducible and upstream releases arrive as reviewable Dependabot PRs
# instead of silent drift.
#
# Deliberately NOT a full transitive lock: only the direct dev tools and the
# optional extras' top-level packages are pinned. Unlisted transitive deps
# still float — a full cross-platform lock (3 Python versions × Linux+Windows)
# would need per-environment compiles for marginal extra safety.
#
# Runtime metadata in pyproject.toml keeps loose lower bounds on purpose
# (maximum install compatibility for users); these pins apply to CI only.
#
# Named requirements-ci.txt (not constraints.txt) so Dependabot's pip
# ecosystem picks it up — see .github/dependabot.yml.

# --- test + type-check tooling ---
pytest==9.0.3
pytest-cov==7.1.0
coverage==7.13.5
# mypy pinned to the version the codebase is verified against; newer releases
# change unused-ignore / attribute checks and flag churn unrelated to the code.
mypy==1.19.1
types-PyYAML==6.0.12.20250915

# --- release tooling (publish.yml) ---
build==1.5.0
twine==6.2.0

# --- runtime dependency ---
PyYAML==6.0.3

# --- optional extras (top-level packages of [live,web,tui,agui,mcp,otel]) ---
rich==14.2.0
textual==8.0.2
fastapi==0.115.12
uvicorn==0.34.0
ag-ui-protocol==0.1.19
mcp==1.26.0
opentelemetry-api==1.42.1
opentelemetry-sdk==1.42.1
opentelemetry-exporter-otlp==1.42.1
